personaChecks.evidence: Front matter declares 'persona: business'. The entire page lives under business-users/content-hub/ and exclusively describes admin UI procedures for content managers — no developer, admin-server, or architect content present. Single-persona tag is correct.

Security sections use outcome-focused, friendly language throughout. Examples: 'You can control who is allowed to view a content item on the live site using two levels of security' (line 351), 'This allows you to reserve content for particular groups, such as *Premium members* or *Subscribers*' (line 354), 'If you need finer control over who can view a content item' (line 376). No technical jargon — zero mentions of classes, APIs, SDKs, code, or configuration files.

Scanned all lines 349–409 (entire security section) for developer terminology: no backtick code spans, no code blocks, no class/method/namespace/interface references, no API terms. The three-tier access model from code analysis (Public/Secured/SecuredWithRoles) is correctly translated to business language: 'Requires authentication' and 'Member role restriction'.

Attempt 2 replaced 'enable the **Requires authentication** option' with 'select the **Requires authentication** option' (lines 368, 390) and 'disable **Requires authentication**' with 'clear the **Requires authentication** option' (line 398). These changes IMPROVE business-user clarity — 'select' and 'clear' are concrete UI actions that map directly to what the user sees (a checkbox), while 'enable/disable' was more abstract.

Attempt 2 replaced 'the **Security** section' with 'the **Membership access** section' (lines 356, 368, 390, 398). This change is persona-neutral to positive — 'Membership access' is the actual label in the admin UI, so it matches what business users will see on screen. No persona regression.

Attempt 2 replaced 'lock icon ({% icon xp-lock %})' with 'diamond icon ({% icon xp-diamond %})' (line 358). This is an accuracy fix with no persona impact — both are equally accessible descriptions for business users.

Both procedures ('Require authentication' lines 364–370, 'Restrict to member roles' lines 386–394) follow one-action-per-step pattern. Step 4 in 'Restrict' (line 390) is 'select the Requires authentication option' — single action. Step 5 (line 391) is 'Select the member roles' — single action. No merged steps.

Steps follow 'In the [location], [action]' pattern per REC-010: 'In the **Membership access** section, select the **Requires authentication** option' (lines 368, 390). Procedures are ≤6 steps (under 8-step max).

Correct terminology throughout security sections per INV-content-030 and REC-013: 'members' for registered visitors (lines 353, 362, 372, 391, 396, 402), 'Visitors' for anonymous users (line 372), 'signed in' / 'signed-in' as verb/adjective (lines 353, 362, 372, 396, 402). Zero instances of 'log in', 'login', 'sign up', 'signup', 'membership role', or 'user role'.

Two image references included (ContentItemSecuritySettings.jpg line 369, ContentItemSecuritySettingsRoles.jpg line 393) per INV-persona-001 'more screenshots for clarity'. Screenshot for role selection is a placeholder per writer notes — acceptable for review, needs actual capture before publish.

The prerequisite callout 'Before you can restrict content items to member roles, your project must have member roles set up' (lines 378–382) correctly delegates setup to technical colleagues per INV-persona-002, cross-referencing the Members page without explaining how to create roles in developer/admin detail.

classificationCheck.evidence: Page contains goal-oriented procedures for specific tasks (create, edit, move, secure, publish, translate, delete content items). The 'Secure content items' section provides step-by-step procedures assuming existing knowledge of the Content hub. This is a howto, not a tutorial (no learning journey) or concept (not purely explanatory).

consistencyCheck.notes: All sibling pages in business-users/content-hub/ (content-hub-folders.md, content-item-assets.md, content-hub.md) target the business persona with the same howto structure — procedural content with admin UI screenshots, one-action-per-step. This page is fully consistent with the cluster. No audience mismatch.

invariantResults.evidence: Business-friendly tone throughout. One action per step in both security procedures. No technical jargon. Screenshot placeholders included for both security setting states.
Prerequisites callout (lines 378–382) assumes technical team handles member role setup. Cross-references to Members page for role creation rather than explaining admin/developer setup inline.

Vocabulary is adjusted to business audience. Uses 'content item', 'member roles', 'signed in', 'Membership access section' — all UI-facing terms. No developer terms like class names, method signatures, or configuration keys.
All steps in both procedures contain exactly one action each, as required for business users.
Correct user-type terminology: 'member' for registered visitors, 'Visitors' for anonymous users. No confusion between member/visitor/user/contact.

summary.attempt2Notes: All three fix categories (select/clear, Membership access, diamond icon) are persona-neutral to positive. No regressions introduced.
